prd.title: Feature Importance for Latent Multiblock and Cross-Block Models

summary.problem: Users need feature-level importance summaries for one-block, cross-block, and future multiblock latent models, aggregated across selected components or subspace units, without running a second bootstrap.
summary.solution: Add feature importance as a design-aware bootstrap artifact consumer for descriptive estimates, intervals, ranks, and top-m frequencies. Add formal p-values later through the existing adapter null_action Monte Carlo path, not through the bootstrap distribution.
non_goals: Do not add feature_importance to the frozen infer_result schema in the first milestone.
Do not expose p-values for unsigned bootstrap importance in the first milestone.
Do not claim subject- or stratum-valid intervals until bootstrap_fits uses design-aware row draws.
Do not expose predictive_gain weights unless an adapter-level per-component gain API exists.

current_architecture: bootstrap_fits produces a multifer_bootstrap_artifact containing per-replicate aligned_loadings by domain.
variable_stability_from_bootstrap, score_stability_from_bootstrap, and subspace_stability_from_bootstrap already consume the artifact as sidecar stability consumers.
infer_result has a fixed schema centered on units; adding a feature_importance table is a schema change.
Adapters already expose null_action in the contract, and engines use this for Monte Carlo null draws.
statistical_principles: Bootstrap replicates estimate uncertainty and stability around the fitted analysis pipeline.
Unsigned importance is nonnegative, so ordinary bootstrap distributions should not be interpreted as null distributions for p-values.
Formal p-values for importance require a design-valid null action or permutation/randomization path.
Signed bootstrap summaries are meaningful only in the component orientation defined by sign or Procrustes alignment to the original fit.

users_and_use_cases.user: Analyst fitting PCA, PLSC, CCA, PLSR, or future multiblock latent models
users_and_use_cases.goal: Rank features by overall contribution across selected latent units and quantify rank stability.
Reuse the existing bootstrap artifact and null_action machinery without duplicating refits unnecessarily.

users_and_use_cases.user: Analyst with clustered or stratified rows
users_and_use_cases.goal: Obtain feature importance intervals that respect subject, cluster, or stratum exchangeability.

functional.description: Provide a public sidecar function feature_importance_from_bootstrap that consumes an existing multifer_bootstrap_artifact and units table.
Compute per-unit feature importance from aligned loadings using squared loadings for singleton units and row-wise squared norm across members for subspace units.
Support aggregate importance across units using k = selected, k = all, or an explicit unit selection.
Define k = selected as the subset of units where units$selected is TRUE, not as an independent scalar rank estimate.
Support initial unit weights equal, root, and root2, with root weights supplied or derived only when available from the fit/artifact context.
Return descriptive bootstrap summaries: estimate, lower, upper, standard error, stability, rank_mean, rank_lower, rank_upper, and top_m_frequency when requested.
Do not include a p_value column in the Phase 1 bootstrap sidecar table.
Record method metadata that clearly labels Phase 1 output as descriptive bootstrap evidence, not formal significance.
Add design-aware row resampling to bootstrap_fits before documenting clustered or stratified importance intervals as valid.
Add optional null-calibrated importance p-values in a later function using adapter$null_action and Monte Carlo p-value calculation.

non_functional.description: The sidecar consumer must not trigger a second bootstrap.
The implementation must preserve the current infer_result schema until an explicit schema review.
Output should be deterministic for a fixed bootstrap artifact.
The API should use the existing domain and unit vocabulary rather than introducing component-only semantics.
The implementation should avoid adapter-specific branching except through explicit adapter capabilities or supplied metadata.

signature.k: one of selected, all, or explicit unit ids/indices
signature.weights: one of equal, root, root2, or numeric vector matching units
signature.roots: optional numeric vector used by root/root2 weighting
signature.top_m: optional positive integer for top-m frequency
signature.probs: numeric length-2 interval probabilities, default c(0.025, 0.975)

signature.observed_importance: output from feature_importance_from_bootstrap or observed-fit calculation
signature.B: positive integer Monte Carlo draw count

aggregate_importance.formula: I[j] = sum_u alpha[u] * q[j,u] / sum_u alpha[u]

normalization.block: Normalize within each domain so importances sum to one for that domain when possible.
normalization.global: Normalize across all domains so importances sum to one when possible.

design_aware_resampling.motivation: Feature importance intervals inherit the validity of the bootstrap artifact. Current iid row bootstrap is not valid for clustered subject designs or fixed-stratum designs.

draw_types.draw: one shared iid row draw applied to every row-aligned domain
stratified row bootstrap preserving stratum sizes
cluster bootstrap sampling subjects with replacement and carrying all rows per subject
cluster bootstrap sampling subjects within strata
apply the existing residual or exchangeability-preserving transform first, then draw within valid exchangeability groups

artifact_extension.resample_indices: Retain the source row indices used by the replicate.
artifact_extension.resample_map: Optional future data frame with output_row, source_row, unit, and stratum.
artifact_extension.resample_weights: Optional future frequency weights for adapters that can consume weighted resamples.

signed_directional_summary.condition: Only for signed quantities after explicit sign or Procrustes alignment to original_fit.
signed_directional_summary.caveat: The result measures concentration relative to the original orientation frame, not orientation-free sign significance.

null_calibrated_unsigned_pvalues.method: Use adapter$null_action to generate null fits, recompute the same importance statistic, and calculate Phipson-Smyth Monte Carlo p-values.

milestones.name: Design-aware bootstrap substrate
deliverables: Introduce an internal resampling-plan helper used by bootstrap_fits.
Preserve current iid behavior for exchangeable_rows and paired_rows.
Add tests for blocked/stratified and clustered draw semantics before exposing clustered claims.
exit_criteria: Existing bootstrap tests pass unchanged for iid and paired cases.
New tests show that grouped/clustered draws preserve the intended row structure.
The artifact records enough draw provenance for later diagnostics.

milestones.name: Descriptive feature-importance sidecar
deliverables: Support per-unit and aggregate selected/all importance.
Return intervals, standard errors, stability, ranks, and optional top-m frequency.
Document that output is descriptive bootstrap evidence with no p-values.
exit_criteria: Unit tests cover oneblock and cross artifacts.
Subspace units use row-wise squared norms across member components.

milestones.name: Observed-fit and weighting refinement
deliverables: Allow observed-fit estimates to be used as point estimates when original loadings are supplied.
Gate root and root2 weights on available root metadata.
Leave predictive_gain out unless an adapter capability supplies per-component gains.
exit_criteria: Weighting errors are explicit when required metadata is missing.
PCA/PLSC/CCA do not expose predictive_gain by default.
Numeric custom weights are validated against unit selection.

milestones.name: Null-calibrated importance p-values
deliverables: Support unadjusted and maxT adjusted Monte Carlo p-values.
Use the same importance statistic as the descriptive sidecar.
Record null_label and validity_level from the recipe/adapter context.
exit_criteria: Null p-values do not depend on bootstrap replicates.
maxT adjustment is tested on a small deterministic fixture.
Design restrictions are inherited from the recipe and adapter null_action.

milestones.name: Schema review for infer integration
deliverables: Decide whether infer_result gets a feature_importance field.
If accepted, add constructor, print method support, docs, and target validation.
If rejected, keep sidecar API as the stable public surface.
exit_criteria: Validity-level enum is not extended accidentally; any new value is deliberate and tested.

tests: A toy artifact with known loadings gives exact squared-loading importance.
A subspace unit with two members gives rowSums(loadings^2).
block normalization sums to one within each domain when total importance is positive.
Bootstrap lower and upper match existing quantile helper behavior.
rank_mean and rank intervals are deterministic for tied and untied examples.
top_m_frequency is NA when top_m is NULL and valid in [0,1] otherwise.
paired_rows applies the same draw to X and Y.
clustered rows duplicate or omit whole clusters, not isolated rows.
clustered plus strata samples clusters within each stratum.
Null action is called exactly B times.
maxT adjusted p-values use the maximum feature statistic per null draw.
No bootstrap artifact is required for p-value calculation.

documentation_plan: Add an Rd page for feature_importance_from_bootstrap documenting descriptive-only interpretation.
Add examples for oneblock PCA and cross-block PLSC/CCA once design-aware bootstrap is in place.
Add a cautionary paragraph that rank intervals and top-m frequencies are stability summaries, not p-values.
Document that k = selected means units flagged selected by the ladder.
Document that clustered or stratified validity depends on the design supplied to infer/bootstrap_fits.

risks_and_mitigations.risk: Users interpret bootstrap rank intervals or top-m frequencies as significance.
risks_and_mitigations.mitigation: Do not include p_value in the Phase 1 table; label method as bootstrap_descriptive; document rank summaries as descriptive only.

risks_and_mitigations.risk: Clustered designs get overly narrow intervals if iid bootstrap remains the only draw path.
risks_and_mitigations.mitigation: Make design-aware bootstrap the first public milestone before documenting clustered claims.

risks_and_mitigations.risk: Predictive-gain weighting is requested for models where it is undefined.
risks_and_mitigations.mitigation: Exclude predictive_gain from Phase 1 and require explicit adapter capability before adding it.

risks_and_mitigations.risk: Adding validity_level = bootstrap breaks existing validation.
risks_and_mitigations.mitigation: Do not use bootstrap as a validity_level unless the enum is deliberately extended; use method metadata instead.

risks_and_mitigations.risk: Selected-unit semantics are confused with a scalar rank k.
risks_and_mitigations.mitigation: Name and document k = selected as units$selected == TRUE; support explicit unit selection separately.

open_questions: Should clustered_rows be a new design constructor or represented as a generalized blocked_rows variant with a sampling_unit field?
Should root metadata be added to bootstrap artifacts, inferred from original_fit, or passed explicitly into feature_importance_from_bootstrap?
Should the Phase 1 sidecar include both per-unit and aggregate rows in one table, or return separate tables?
Should null-calibrated p-values refit full models for every null draw, or use adapter core/update hooks where valid?
Should a future infer_result schema change include feature importance by default or only when requested as a target?
